sanskrit:
एवं परम्पराप्राप्तमिमं राजर्षयो विदुः।
स कालेनेह महता योगो नष्टः परन्तप॥
transliteration:
evaṃ paramparā-prāptam imaṃ rājarṣayo viduḥ
sa kāleneha mahatā yogo naṣṭaḥ parantapa
literal_meaning: Thus, received through disciplic succession, this (knowledge) the royal sages understood; that (succession) in course of time here by great (lapse) the yoga was lost, O scorcher of foes.
contextual_meaning: A once-living wisdom tradition can ossify into dead ritual or be hijacked by personal agendas; when the chain of living transmission breaks, the inner science of Self-realization appears lost, producing collective amnesia and existential anxiety.
psychological_state: Cultural PTSD: a gut-level sense that something vital once known has vanished, leaving a vacuum filled by superficial substitutes and dogma.
trigger_scenario: A graduate student realizes their entire psychology syllabus is 100-year-old translations filtered through colonial biases; the original experiential practices were never passed on, triggering panic that ‘real’ knowledge is gone.
anchor_text: Keep wisdom alive by passing it on with integrity.
